caseStudies.title: ML Journey: End-to-End Prediction Platform
caseStudies.subtitle: A scalable machine learning platform for real-time predictions.
caseStudies.problem: Data scientists often struggle with deploying models efficiently. There is a gap between a Jupyter notebook and a scalable production endpoint.
caseStudies.approach: Built a seamless deployment pipeline using Streamlit and Docker, allowing models to be containerized and exposed via REST APIs with zero downtime.
caseStudies.results: Reduced deployment time from days to minutes, successfully serving over 10,000 requests with sub-50ms latency.

caseStudies.title: GenAI Agent with Langchain
caseStudies.subtitle: Autonomous conversational agent with RAG capabilities.
caseStudies.problem: Standard LLMs hallucinate on proprietary data and lack the ability to perform complex, multi-step actions across various toolsets.
caseStudies.approach: Implemented a Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) architecture using LangChain, integrating vector databases for semantic search and function calling.
caseStudies.results: Achieved a 40% reduction in hallucination rates and enabled the agent to autonomously query databases and summarize financial documents.

global.heroDescription: Crafting production-grade AI systems and highly scalable ML infrastructure. Bridging the gap between cutting-edge research and real-world deployment.
global.aboutText1: I am an AI Systems Engineer focused on bridging the gap between theoretical machine learning and robust production environments. My work revolves around training, fine-tuning, and deploying large-scale language models (LLMs) and computer vision systems.
global.aboutText2: I believe that the future of software isn't just about writing code; it's about orchestrating intelligence. This requires a deep understanding of both distributed systems and neural architectures.

expertise.title: Transformers
expertise.description: Attention mechanisms and large scale models.

certificates.title: Coursera certifies their successful completion of Supervised Machine Learning: Regression and Classification
